---
title: "Use Cases"
subtitle: "Browse datasets by potential research application"
---

<div class="page-intro">
This page organizes datasets by potential research use case based on available registry tags. <strong>This page does not recommend, rank, or endorse any dataset.</strong> Entries listed under each use case are tagged as potentially relevant based on publicly indexed source metadata and conservative registry annotations. Suitability for any specific analysis must be assessed by the researcher based on study design, sample size, platform, access status, and metadata completeness.
</div>


---

## Adult vs. Pediatric Comparisons

<div class="use-case-section">
<div class="uc-note">These datasets are tagged as potentially relevant to this use case based on available registry annotations. Pediatric cohorts are specifically labeled where known; adult designation reflects the majority of unlabeled cohorts but should be verified.</div>
</div>

<div data-registry-table data-flag="usable_for_pediatric_analysis" data-rows="12"></div>


---

## Mucosal Disease-vs-Control Transcriptomics

<div class="use-case-section">
<div class="uc-note">Datasets with intestinal mucosal biopsies from IBD and non-IBD controls, potentially useful for disease-vs-control transcriptomic analyses. Tissue site (ileum vs. colon vs. rectum) and platform should be verified before pooling studies.</div>
</div>

<div data-registry-table data-flag="usable_for_disease_vs_control" data-rows="12"></div>


---

## Blood-Based Transcriptomics

<div class="use-case-section">
<div class="uc-note">Datasets using whole blood or peripheral blood as the source tissue. Blood-based transcriptomics is sensitive to cell composition variability and should be interpreted with attention to disease activity, treatment status, and cell-type deconvolution.</div>
</div>

<div data-registry-table data-any="blood|PBMC|methylation" data-rows="12"></div>


---

## CD vs. UC Comparison

<div class="use-case-section">
<div class="uc-note">Datasets including both Crohn disease and ulcerative colitis samples, potentially relevant for direct CD-vs-UC comparisons. Tissue site matching between CD and UC samples is important for valid comparisons.</div>
</div>

<div data-registry-table data-flag="usable_for_cd_vs_uc" data-rows="12"></div>


---

## Disease Activity and Longitudinal Analysis

<div class="use-case-section">
<div class="uc-note">Datasets with disease activity information or longitudinal follow-up, potentially relevant for studying dynamic changes, remission, or treatment response over time. Longitudinal datasets are particularly valuable but also more complex to analyze.</div>
</div>

<div data-registry-table data-flag="usable_for_longitudinal_analysis" data-columns="dataset_name,accession,omics_layer,disease,sample_type,data_access_status,longitudinal" data-rows="12"></div>


---

## Cell-Type Reference and Deconvolution

<div class="use-case-section">
<div class="uc-note">Single-cell and reference datasets potentially useful for building or applying cell-type deconvolution models to bulk transcriptomic data. Cell annotation quality and tissue site matching to the bulk dataset should be considered carefully.</div>
</div>

<div data-registry-table data-flag="usable_for_deconvolution_reference" data-rows="12"></div>


---

## Spatial Tissue Organization

<div class="use-case-section">
<div class="uc-note">Datasets using spatial transcriptomics to characterize the spatial organization of cell types and gene expression within IBD tissue sections. Platform-specific resolution and gene panel coverage should be considered.</div>
</div>

<div data-registry-table data-any="spatial|cosmx|visium|xenium|spatial transcriptomics" data-rows="10"></div>


---

## Treatment Response Analysis

<div class="use-case-section">
<div class="uc-note">Datasets with treatment metadata or specifically designed to study treatment response. These are potentially relevant for analyzing biological correlates of response or non-response to IBD therapies. Treatment status at specimen collection and response definitions vary across studies.</div>
</div>

<div data-registry-table data-flag="usable_for_treatment_response" data-columns="dataset_name,accession,omics_layer,disease,sample_type,data_access_status,treatment_metadata" data-rows="12"></div>


---

## Host–Microbiome Integration

<div class="use-case-section">
<div class="uc-note">Datasets including microbiome or multi-omics data relevant to host–microbiome research in IBD. Integration of host and microbial data requires careful sample-level matching and statistical methods accounting for compositionality and sparsity.</div>
</div>

<div data-registry-table data-flag="usable_for_host_microbiome" data-rows="12"></div>


---

## Fecal Proteomics and Metaproteomics

<div class="use-case-section">
<div class="uc-note">Datasets using stool-derived proteomics or metaproteomics, capturing both host-derived and microbial proteins non-invasively. Fecal proteomics is sensitive to sample handling and storage conditions.</div>
</div>

<div data-registry-table data-any="fecal|stool|proteomics|metaproteomics" data-rows="12"></div>


---

## Metabolomics and Lipidomics

<div class="use-case-section">
<div class="uc-note">Datasets measuring small molecules or lipids in IBD-relevant samples. Platform and sample matrix (stool, blood, urine, tissue) substantially affect what is measured and how results should be interpreted.</div>
</div>

<div data-registry-table data-any="metabolomics|lipidomics|metabolite" data-rows="12"></div>


---

## DNA Methylation and Epigenomics

<div class="use-case-section">
<div class="uc-note">Datasets measuring DNA methylation or chromatin state in IBD-relevant tissues. Cell composition confounding in blood samples and probe coverage limitations in older arrays (450K) are important considerations.</div>
</div>

<div data-registry-table data-any="methylation|epigenomics|ewas|epigenetic" data-rows="12"></div>


---

## Genetics and Target Prioritization

<div class="use-case-section">
<div class="uc-note">Genetic resources including GWAS summary statistics and individual-level genotype data. Useful for genetic risk factor mapping, colocalization, Mendelian randomization, and target prioritization. Individual-level data are largely controlled-access.</div>
</div>

<div data-registry-table data-flag="usable_for_genetics_target_prioritization" data-columns="dataset_name,accession,omics_layer,disease,sample_type,data_access_status,controlled_access" data-rows="12"></div>


---

<div class="disclaimer">
<strong>Note.</strong> Use-case groupings are based on registry tags in the registry CSV and are designed to assist in dataset discovery only. They do not constitute a recommendation that any listed dataset is suitable for the stated use case. Researchers should independently verify data quality, study design, sample characteristics, and access conditions before initiating any analysis.
</div>
